channel.title: NYT > World News
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Israel Attacks Beirut and Tehran as Fighting in Middle East Accelerates
item.description: The Israeli military said it had begun a “broad-scale” attack in Iran’s capital, hours after hitting Hezbollah strongholds in Lebanon. The Pentagon said Iran’s retaliatory missile strikes were decreasing.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times

item.title: Iran’s Friends Include China and Russia. But Where are They Now?
item.description: Iran maintains ties with a range of countries, including Turkey, India, Russia and China. Yet in this war, their support is mostly rhetoric.
item.dc:creator: Ben Hubbard
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A police station that was destroyed by U.S.-Israeli airstrikes in Tehran.

item.title: South African President on Trump’s Oval Office ‘Ambush’ and ‘Racist’ Policies
item.description: In an interview with The New York Times, President Cyril Ramaphosa opened up about the role of middle powers, relations with Washington and apartheid.
item.dc:creator: John Eligon and Zimasa Matiwane
item.media:credit: Joao Silva/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Cyril Ramaphosa of South Africa opened up about his relationship with President Trump.

item.title: James Luckey-Lange Recounts Being Detained, Starved and Beaten in Venezuela
item.description: James Luckey-Lange, 28, wrote about kindness and shared humanity as he traveled. But he said he had been shackled, starved and beaten in Venezuela after being detained.
item.dc:creator: Annie Correal and Anatoly Kurmanaev
item.category: Goodbye Horses: The Many Lives of Q Lazzarus (Movie)
item.media:credit: Hannah Yoon for The New York Times
item.media:description: James Luckey-Lange was held by Venezuelan authorities for more than a month and was released after then President Nicolás Maduro was captured in January.

item.title: Israel Pushes Farther Into Lebanon, and Readies for More
item.description: Days into its offensive against Hezbollah, Israel is massing armored vehicles near the Lebanese border for a potentially much larger ground incursion.
item.dc:creator: David M. Halbfinger and Natan Odenheimer
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Avishag Shaar-Yashuv for The New York Times
item.media:description: Military trucks transporting tanks toward the Israeli-Lebanese border on Thursday.

item.title: After Gen-Z Revolution, Nepal Votes for a Fresh Slate of Leaders
item.description: A millennial rapper and former mayor is aiming to lead the country, six months after the old guard was ousted.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Beech, Binod Ghimire, Sajal Pradhan and Hannah Reyes Morales
item.media:description: Voting in Kathmandu, Nepal, on Thursday. The city still bears the scars of last year’s Gen Z-led revolution

item.title: Who Is Eyal Zamir, the Architect of Israel’s War Plans?
item.description: The Israeli general has long been focused on the threat from Iran and its proxies, and has called for a regional alliance against the country.
item.dc:creator: Adam Rasgon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Amit Elkayam for The New York Times
item.media:description: Lt. Gen. Eyal Zamir, the Israeli military’s chief of staff, attending a ceremony in Jerusalem in October.

item.title: Israel Closes Al Aqsa Mosque at Ramadan, Citing Wartime Safety Concerns
item.description: The temporary measure, during the holy Muslim fasting month, follows tensions as Jewish activists increasingly encroach on the contested religious site.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Kershner
item.media:credit: Ammar Awad/Reuters
item.media:description: An missile fired to intercept incoming fire flying over Jerusalem on Sunday, with the Al-Aqsa Mosque compound visible in the foreground.

item.title: China Bets on Technology to Resist U.S. Pressure
item.description: China announced a 7 percent increase in military spending and a five-year plan to try to reduce its military and industry’s reliance on Western technology.
item.dc:creator: Chris Buckley and Lily Kuo
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Lintao Zhang/Getty Images
item.media:description: Xi Jinping, China’s leader, at the annual meeting of the national legislature in Beijing on Thursday.

item.title: Australia’s Sydney Jewish Museum to Memorialize Bondi Beach Massacre
item.description: The country and its small Jewish community are still trying to process the mass shooting at Bondi Beach in December.
item.dc:creator: Laura Chung and Matthew Abbott
item.media:credit: Matthew Abbott for The New York Times

item.title: Refugee Sentenced to 13 Years for Stabbing at Berlin Holocaust Memorial
item.description: A German court found that the attacker, now age 20, had traveled more than 100 miles to stab a Spanish tourist, who survived.
item.dc:creator: Christopher F. Schuetze
item.media:credit: Christopher Neundorf/EPA, via Shutterstock
item.media:description: The Holocaust Memorial was cordoned off after a stabbing attack last year.

item.title: Why Ecuador Invited the U.S. Military to Help With Its Drug Gangs
item.description: Drug gangs have turned the South American country into one of the most dangerous in the region and the world’s leading exporter of cocaine.
item.dc:creator: Luis Ferré-Sadurní, Eric Schmitt and José María León Cabrera
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Daniele Volpe for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Ecuadorean Coast Guard patrolling in 2025. The U.S. military is now helping Ecuador plan a series of raids targeting the South American country’s violent drug gangs.

item.title: Global Leaders Brace for the Fallout From a Fast Metastasizing War
item.description: Higher energy prices, political instability and a potential new wave of refugees: The escalating regional conflict in the Middle East could have far-reaching effects.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A woman cries after seeing the rubble from a building that was hit by airstrikes in Tehran on Tuesday.

item.title: Where Are China’s A.I. Doomers?
item.description: Chinese policymakers and the public have expressed high levels of optimism about A.I., even as many in the West worry about the technology’s effects on employment or humanity in general.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Wang
item.media:credit: Qilai Shen for The New York Times
item.media:description: Service robots on display at the World Artificial Intelligence Conference in Shanghai last year.

item.title: 3 Australians Were on U.S. Submarine that Sank an Iranian Warship
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese’s disclosure about the attack in the Indian Ocean prompted concern that Australia’s close military cooperation with the United States could draw it into the fighting in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Victoria Kim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Thilina Kaluthotage/Reuters
item.media:description: An injured Iranian sailor is moved on a stretcher at Galle National Hospital in Sri Lanka after the torpedoing of the Iranian naval ship IRIS Dena on Thursday.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward, Erika Solomon, Dayana Iwaza and Ephrat Livni

item.title: As Trump Out-Putins Putin, Russia’s Global Influence Erodes
item.description: The conflict in Iran may give Moscow a short-term boost economically and in Ukraine. But it has also shown the limits of Russia’s partnerships.
item.dc:creator: Paul Sonne
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Pool photo by Maxim Shipenkov
item.media:description: President Vladimir V. Putin of Russia went to a wreath-laying ceremony at the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier in Moscow last month.

item.title: Jamil Jivani, a Friend of JD Vance, Channels Charlie Kirk With Young Men
item.description: Jamil Jivani, a Conservative rookie member of Parliament, follows an American playbook to win over young men on college campuses.
item.dc:creator: Norimitsu Onishi
item.media:credit: Gavin John for The New York Times
item.media:description: Jamil Jivani, a conservative member of Parliament, talking with supporters after speaking at the University of Calgary last month.

item.title: He Had a Purple Heart, PTSD and a Rap Sheet. He Had to Leave the U.S.
item.description: After a long battle with drug addiction, Sae Joon Park felt settled in a new life. But he was deported last year and is now fighting to get back to Hawaii.
item.dc:creator: Max Kim
item.media:credit: Jean Chung for The New York Times
item.media:description: Sae Joon Park in his apartment in Busan, South Korea, in February. Despite living in the U.S. for nearly 50 years, Park was ordered to move back to South Korea in June 2025.

item.title: The Good Times Are Over in China’s Liquor Capital
item.description: Once celebrated for its fiery spirits, the town of Maotai has reeled from a bad Chinese economy, changing tastes and a crackdown on boozy official banquets.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Higgins and Gilles Sabrié
item.media:description: Tanks of fermented sorghum at Jinjiao Distillery in Maotai, China, in February.

item.title: U.S. Restarts Diplomatic Relations With Venezuela
item.description: Washington has rapidly warmed up ties with Venezuela — and applied major pressure on it — since capturing the country’s president, Nicolás Maduro, two months ago.
item.dc:creator: Max Bearak
item.media:credit: Adriana Loureiro Fernandez for The New York Times
item.media:description: A protest last month in Caracas, Venezuela’s capital, demanding freedom for political prisoners.

item.title: Israel Pounds Southern Beirut, a Hezbollah Stronghold
item.description: The strikes were the most intense bombardment since a cease-fire deal in late 2024.
item.dc:creator: Dayana Iwaza, Euan Ward, Hwaida Saad and Ephrat Livni
item.media:credit: Hassan Ammar/Associated Press
item.media:description: Smoke rising after an Israeli airstrike in the southern suburbs of Beirut, Lebanon, on early Friday.

item.title: State Department Is Accused of Slow Assistance in Iran War for Americans Abroad
item.description: Many veteran diplomats faulted the State Department for its response after the attacks in Iran began, and for its actions beforehand.
item.dc:creator: Michael Crowley and Edward Wong
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Kevin Lamarque/Reuters
item.media:description: Passengers evacuated from the Middle East arrived at Dulles International Airport on Thursday.

item.title: Not Just Oil: In Venezuela, U.S. Interior Secretary Pushes for Mining Access
item.description: Venezuela is home to large reserves of rare earths, gold and other valuable minerals that the Trump administration wants to exert more control over.
item.dc:creator: Max Bearak and Lisa Friedman
item.media:credit: Federico Parra/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Interior Secretary Doug Burgum and Delcy Rodríguez, Venezuela’s new leader, in Caracas on Wednesday.

item.title: Superfood Fuels Mating Frenzy for Critically Endangered Kakapo
item.description: Kakapos, which are reclusive and flightless and can live as long as humans, are found only in New Zealand. They feed on the fruit of the rimu tree.
item.dc:creator: Neil Vigdor
item.media:credit: Andrew Digby/Dept. of Conservation, New Zealand, via Associated Press
item.media:description: A kakapo with eggs on Anchor Island, in New Zealand. The island is known for lacking the kinds of predators that threaten the parrots.

item.title: Two Schools in Iran Damaged in U.S.-Israeli Bombing
item.description: Photos show that both schools, southeast of Tehran, sustained damage. No injuries were reported.
item.dc:creator: Christiaan Triebert and Parin Behrooz
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Atta Kenare/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: The aftermath of a strike in Tehran, the Iranian capital, on Thursday.

item.title: Iran delays naming a new leader out of security concerns, officials say.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.media:credit: Rouzbeh Fouladi/Middle East Images, via Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Mojtaba Khamenei, center, the son of the Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, participates in the annual Quds Day rally in Tehran, Iran, in 2019.

item.title: Israel Begins Assault in Southern Beirut, Raising Fears of Wider Incursion
item.description: As the war with Iran threatens to engulf more countries in the region, President Trump said he wanted to be involved in picking a new leader in Tehran.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward, Aurelien Breeden, Erika Solomon and Thomas Fuller
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: Smoke rising after an Israeli airstrike in the southern suburbs of Beirut, Lebanon, late Thursday and early Friday local time.

item.title: António Lobo Antunes, One of Europe’s Most Revered Writers, Dies at 83
item.description: In a career studded with literary awards, he was the author of dozens of books that grappled with his nation’s legacy of dictatorship and colonialism.
item.dc:creator: Adam Nossiter
item.media:credit: Ulf Andersen/Getty Images
item.media:description: António Lobo Antunes in 1988. The literary critic George Steiner called him “a novelist of the very first rank” and likened him to Joseph Conrad and William Faulkner.

item.title: A Different Kind of War in Mexico
item.description: Our correspondents discuss the challenges of Mexico’s war on drug cartels.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Cristopher Rogel Blanquet for The New York Times

item.title: Why Torpedoed Iranian Warship Is a Problem for India
item.description: An Iranian ship had been invited by India to take part in peacetime naval exercises with dozens of other countries, days before it was sunk in a U.S. submarine attack.
item.dc:creator: Alex Travelli and Suhasini Raj
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Associated Press
item.media:description: The Iranian warship, IRIS Dena, in the Bay of Bengal during an international fleet review at Visakhapatnam, India, on Feb. 18.

item.title: Analysis Suggests School Was Hit Amid U.S. Strikes on Iranian Naval Base
item.description: The Feb. 28 school strike in Minab, which killed dozens, including children, appears to have been part of an attack on an adjacent naval base in southern Iran, where officials said U.S. forces were operating.
item.dc:creator: Malachy Browne and Aaron Boxerman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity
Human Rights and Human Rights Violations
item.media:credit: Planet Labs, Rafaela Balster/The New York Times

item.title: Desperate travelers wait as Dubai resumes a few flights.
item.description: As Iranian missile strikes continue to the U.A.E., stranded passengers at Dubai International Airport anxiously await a way back home.
item.dc:creator: Ismaeel Naar
item.media:credit: Altaf Qadri/Associated Press
item.media:description: Passengers arriving at Dubai International Airport terminal as the airport resumed limited operations in Dubai, United Arab Emirates, on Thursday.

item.title: French Officer Accused of Shooting Teen to Be Charged Only With Manslaughter
item.description: The shooting of Nahel Merzouk in 2023 set off unrest across France, where his treatment was seen as emblematic of police brutality toward ethnic minorities.
item.dc:creator: Catherine Porter and Ana Castelain
item.media:credit: Julien De Rosa/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: A protest in France last year marking the anniversary of the death of Nahel Merzouk.

item.title: Trump Says He Should Have Role in Choosing Iran’s Next Leader
item.description: The president’s comments were the most detailed he has been about his vision of a U.S. role in creating a new government in Tehran.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump said Mojtaba Khamenei, a son of the former Iranian leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei who appeared to be the leading candidate to succeed his father, was an “unacceptable” choice.

item.title: How many Americans live in the Mideast?
item.dc:creator: Amy Schoenfeld Walker
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: The White House press secretary, Karoline Levitt, providing information for American citizens in the Middle East at the White House in Washington on Wednesday.

item.title: Sri Lanka Is Caught in the Middle as Second Iranian Vessel Seeks Safe Haven
item.description: A day after rescuing Iranian sailors from the wreckage of a warship sunk by a U.S. submarine, Sri Lanka’s neutrality is being tested.
item.dc:creator: Anupreeta Das and Pamodi Waravita
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Ishara S. Kodikara/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: A police vehicle arriving at a hospital in Galle, Sri Lanka, on Thursday, where Iranian sailors rescued from the IRIS Dena were being treated.

item.title: Israel expanded its evacuation warnings for Lebanese residents to Beirut’s southern suburbs.
item.dc:creator: Elena Shao

item.title: This is what happened on March 4.
Israeli Evacuation Orders Spark Panic in Beirut
item.description: Lebanon’s capital erupted in chaos after the Israeli military ordered people to flee the southern suburbs. A far-right minister in Israel warned of a devastating bombardment.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward and Dayana Iwaza
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Claudia Greco/Reuters
item.media:description: Jammed roads in Beirut, the capital of Lebanon, after Israel’s military issued evacuation orders of entire neighborhood on Thursday.

item.title: Fueled by anxiety, exhausted Iranians cross the border into Turkey.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.media:credit: Dilara Senkaya/Reuters
item.media:description: Iranians after crossing into Turkey at the Kapikoy Border Gate earlier this week.

item.title: As Iran Widens Retaliatory Strikes, Europe Accelerates Response for Gulf Allies
item.description: Defense Minister Guido Crosetto said that Italy would provide “air defense systems, anti-drone and antimissile systems” as Iran widened its retaliatory strikes.
item.dc:creator: Elisabetta Povoledo and Lizzie Dearden
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Nicolas Tucat/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni said Italy would also honor longstanding bilateral agreements regulating U.S. military bases on Italian soil, as long as they were not used for operations that involved bombing.

item.title: Fallout From Mideast Swirls Over the War in Ukraine
item.description: Three-way peace talks are on hold, and Kyiv fears that U.S. air defense systems it needs to counter Russia could be diverted to the Iran conflict.
item.dc:creator: Cassandra Vinograd
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Tetiana Dzhafarova/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: President Volodymyr Zelensky of Ukraine has offered to share his country’s expertise in defending against Iranian-designed drones.

item.title: Iceland Defeats Iceland: A U.K. Supermarket Ends a Trademark Dispute
item.description: Can a company trademark the name of a country? In the case of Iceland, the British grocery store, the answer is no.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Gross
item.media:credit: Paul Ellis/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: An Iceland supermarket in Liverpool, England. The grocery chain was founded in 1970.

item.title: Iranian Kurdish Forces Say They May Enter Iran. Who Are the Kurds?
item.description: Spread across Iran, Iraq, Syria and Turkey, the ethnic group of 40 million has long sought, and fought for, greater autonomy.
item.dc:creator: Raja Abdulrahim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Thaier Al-Sudani/Reuters
item.media:description: Iranian Kurdish fighters from the Kurdistan Freedom Party (PAK) take part in a training session at a base on the outskirts of Erbil, Iraq last month.

item.title: NATO’s chief downplays the risks that the alliance will be drawn into the conflict.
item.dc:creator: Lara Jakes
item.media:credit: Yves Herman/Reuters
item.media:description: The Secretary General of NATO, Mark Rutte, downplayed risks that the military alliance could be pulled into the conflict in the Middle East, on Thursday.

item.title: Israel Says It Downed an Iranian Yak-130 Fighter Jet Over Tehran
item.description: Israel said one of its F-35 fighter jets, known as an Adir, had downed another crewed plane over Tehran, the Iranian capital. Iran did not immediately comment.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Atef Safadi/EPA, via Shutterstock
item.media:description: An Israeli F-35 fighter jet in 2024.

item.title: Trump Says He Wants to Destroy Iran’s Navy. Here’s What to Know.
item.description: The United States has long considered Iranian naval ships a serious threat, even as the country’s nuclear and missile programs dominate discussions of its military capabilities.
item.dc:creator: Qasim Nauman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Morteza Nikoubazl/NurPhoto, via Getty Images
item.media:description: Iranian naval vessels during a parade in 2024.

item.title: Iranian Drones Fall in Azerbaijan as Conflict Spreads Beyond Mideast
item.description: Azerbaijan threatened to retaliate, as the neighbors with a long history of tensions found themselves again at odds.
item.dc:creator: Ivan Nechepurenko and Sanjana Varghese
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Iran Calls Torpedoing of Ship by U.S. an ‘Atrocity’
item.description: Iran’s foreign minister accused the United States of committing an “atrocity at sea,” after a U.S. Navy submarine attacked an Iranian frigate in the Indian Ocean.
item.dc:creator: Max Kim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Eranga Jayawardene/Associated Press
item.media:description: Workers unloading the bodies of Iranian sailors killed when an American submarine torpedoed and sank an Iranian frigate off the coast of Sri Lanka.

item.title: Trump Wants a Quick Victory in Iran. But the War May Be Costly.
item.description: The opening days of the conflict are challenging the idea that President Trump can project force abroad while safeguarding American lives and the economy.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Heavy smoke rising from Tehran on Tuesday after U.S.-Israeli airstrikes.

item.title: Carney Says Canada and Australia Can ‘Set the Agenda’ as World Faces ‘Crises’
item.description: The Canadian prime minister is touring the Asia-Pacific to rejuvenate alliances in the wake of the U.S. trade war and President Trump’s calls for Canada’s annexation.
item.dc:creator: Ian Austen
item.media:credit: David Gray/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Prime Minister Mark Carney of Canada addressing Parliament in Canberra, Australia, on Thursday.

item.title: Here is the latest.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward, Erika Solomon, Dayana Iwaza and Ephrat Livni

item.title: On the Ground in Beirut
item.description: Our Beirut bureau chief, Christina Goldbaum, reports on the escalating conflict between Israel and the Iran-backed armed group Hezbollah, as Israel’s military seizes areas of southern Lebanon and carries out bombings.
item.dc:creator: Christina Goldbaum, Christina Thornell, Jon Miller, James Surdam and Diego Ibarra Sánchez for The New York Times
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Man Accused of Plotting to Kill Trump Blames Iran for Scheme
item.description: Asif Merchant testified in his own defense, saying he participated in the plot to protect his family in Iran. Prosecutors reject his account of his motives.
item.dc:creator: Santul Nerkar
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: A plot to kill President Trump or former President Joseph R. Biden Jr. had little chance of success, a man charged with terrorism and murder for hire testified.

item.title: Experts doubt Tehran’s claims of advanced ‘hypersonic’ missiles.
item.description: As Iran fires fewer ballistic missiles, experts doubt Tehran is saving more advanced weapons.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Morteza Nikoubazl
item.media:description: A military parade in Iran in 2024. Tehran has said that it has developed hypersonic missile technology, but many security experts doubt that the missiles have such capacity.

item.title: Pro-American Kurdish Forces Are Preparing Possible Iran Incursion
item.description: The C.I.A. has given small weapons to Iranian Kurdish forces in Iraq in a covert program that began before the current war.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon, Julian E. Barnes, Christiaan Triebert, Parin Behrooz and Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Safin Hamid/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: A member of the Kurdistan Democratic Party of Iran inspects the damage sustained at the party’s Azadi Camp after an Iranian cross-border attack in the Kurdish region of northern Iraq.

item.title: Israelis Celebrate Purim Amid Iranian Missile Attacks
item.description: The war did not stop ultra-Orthodox parts of Jerusalem from drinking and dancing for the Jewish holiday.
item.dc:creator: Natan Odenheimer
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Avishag Shaar-Yashuv for The New York Times
item.media:description: Celebrating Purim inside a bomb shelter in Tel Aviv on Monday.

item.title: As Attacks Spread, War With Iran Reaches Far Beyond Mideast
item.description: NATO downed a missile fired by Iran at Turkey, and a U.S. sub sank an Iranian Navy ship off Sri Lanka, thousands of miles from the war zone.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt, Helene Cooper, Lara Jakes and Michael Levenson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Damage in Tehran has been extensive, including at the police station in the background on Wednesday.

item.title: Senate Thwarts Bid to Curb Trump’s War Powers on Iran
item.description: Nearly every Republican voted to block a measure that would require that President Trump win authorization from Congress to continue the offensive in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Robert Jimison
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: The rubble of a police station in Tehran this week after the U.S.-Israeli airstrikes.
